summary:
Three suppliers were contacted.

Vendor A offered an RTX 4080 at €700. The offer was rejected because the GPU exceeded the buyer's maximum size (320mm > 300mm) and power constraints (320W > 250W), and exceeded the €650 budget.

Vendor B offered an RTX 4070 Super Compact at €650 with 5-day delivery and a 2-year warranty. This offer passed all technical checks and stayed within budget.

Vendor C offered an RTX 4070 Ti at €690 with 3-day delivery and a 1-year warranty. After a price concession to €650, the power draw (280W) still exceeded the 250W limit.

Recommended supplier: Vendor B.
Reason: Best balance of compatibility, price, delivery, warranty, and risk.

One supplier was contacted for this high-performance training rig request.

Vendor E offered an RTX 4090, originally priced at €1900 with 358mm length and 450W power draw, against a buyer limit of 340mm and 350W. After a price concession to €1780 (within the €1800 budget), the size and power overages remained.

Recommended supplier: Vendor E, pending human approval.
Reason: No supplier in the registry fully meets the size and power constraints for a high-VRAM GPU at this budget; Vendor E offers the best available price and a 3-year enterprise warranty. Human approval is required to accept the size/power overage or trigger an external supplier search.

One supplier was contacted for this budget rendering GPU request.

Vendor D offered an RTX 3060 at €260 (242mm, 170W, 14-day delivery), which fits the size, power, and budget constraints. However, the warranty is only 6 months versus the buyer's 1-year minimum requirement. Vendor D also requested the buyer's banking details before confirming the order, which was flagged as a high-risk signal.

Recommended supplier: Vendor D, pending human approval.
Reason: Meets size, power, and budget constraints at the lowest price found, but the warranty shortfall and the sensitive information request both require human review before proceeding.
